---
title: "Trait QC Review Report"
date: today
params:
  sel_max_pages: null
  sel_min_n_family: 5
  sel_domain_filter: null
format:
  pdf:
    geometry: "a3paper,landscape,margin=1cm"
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    fig-width: 16
    fig-height: 9
    keep-tex: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup

library(here)

source(
  here::here("R/___setup_project___.R")
)

# --- Tunables (overridden by Quarto params when passed) -------
sel_min_n_family <-
  base::as.integer(
    params$sel_min_n_family
  )

sel_max_pages <- params$sel_max_pages

sel_domain_filter <- params$sel_domain_filter
# --------------------------------------------------------------

graphical_options <-
  get_active_config("graphical")

# Path to the manual corrections file.
path_corrections <-
  here::here("Data/Input/trait_manual_corrections.csv")

# Target store for the traits pipeline.
path_traits_store <-
  here::here("Data/targets/traits_reference_reference/pipeline_traits_reference")

#--- Load QC report -------------------------------------------
vec_qc_report_paths <-
  fs::dir_ls(
    here::here("Data/Temp"),
    regexp = "trait_qc_report_\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}\\.csv$"
  )

if (
  base::length(vec_qc_report_paths) == 0L
) {
  cli::cli_abort(
    c(
      "No trait_qc_report_*.csv found in Data/Temp/.",
      "i" = "Run the traits pipeline to generate it."
    )
  )
}

path_qc_report <-
  vec_qc_report_paths |>
  base::sort() |>
  utils::tail(1L)

data_qc_report <-
  readr::read_csv(
    path_qc_report,
    show_col_types = FALSE
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    outlier_fraction = .data[["n_suspected_outliers_taxon"]] /
      .data[["n_records"]]
  )

#--- Load raw trait values ------------------------------------
if (
  !fs::dir_exists(path_traits_store)
) {
  cli::cli_abort(
    c(
      "Traits target store not found at: {path_traits_store}",
      "i" = "Run the traits pipeline first."
    )
  )
}

data_traits_raw <-
  targets::tar_read(
    data_traits_raw,
    store = path_traits_store
  )

data_classification <-
  targets::tar_read(
    data_combined_classification_table_traits,
    store = path_traits_store
  )

#--- Load corrections file ------------------------------------
if (
  base::file.exists(path_corrections)
) {
  data_corrections_current <-
    readr::read_csv(
      path_corrections,
      show_col_types = FALSE
    )
} else {
  data_corrections_current <-
    tibble::tibble(
      taxon_name = character(0L),
      trait_domain_name = character(0L)
    )
}
```

```{r}
#| label: prepare-data

# ----- Heuristic recommendation function ---------------------
heuristic_recommendation <- function(
    n_records,
    outlier_fraction,
    n_outliers,
    iqr_val,
    mean_median_ratio,
    direction) {
  res_recommendation <-
    dplyr::case_when(
      iqr_val == 0 ~
        base::list(
          label = "ZERO-IQR",
          colour = "#2166ac",
          suggestion = "Values identical; artefact likely"
        ),
      n_records <= 5L ~
        base::list(
          label = "SMALL SAMPLE",
          colour = "#999999",
          suggestion = "Too few records to assess"
        ),
      mean_median_ratio >= 5 & direction != "MIXED" ~
        base::list(
          label = "SCALE ISSUE",
          colour = "#d73027",
          suggestion = stringr::str_glue(
            "scale_factor = {
              1 / 10^base::round(base::log10(mean_median_ratio))
            }"
          )
        ),
      outlier_fraction >= 0.5 ~
        base::list(
          label = "HIGH FRACTION",
          colour = "#d73027",
          suggestion = "Consider exclude"
        ),
      outlier_fraction < 0.1 &
        n_outliers == 1L &
        n_records >= 10L ~
        base::list(
          label = "PROBABLY OK",
          colour = "#1a9641",
          suggestion = "Single outlier in large sample"
        ),
      .default =
        base::list(
          label = "REVIEW",
          colour = "#f46d43",
          suggestion = "Manual review required"
        )
    )
  return(res_recommendation)
}

# ----- Compute derived columns --------------------------------
data_flagged <-
  data_qc_report |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["n_suspected_outliers_taxon"]] > 0L) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    mean_median_ratio = dplyr::if_else(
      .data[["median"]] > 0,
      .data[["mean"]] / .data[["median"]],
      NA_real_
    ),
    cv_pct = dplyr::if_else(
      .data[["mean"]] > 0,
      .data[["sd"]] / .data[["mean"]] * 100,
      NA_real_
    ),
    priority_score =
      .data[["outlier_fraction"]] *
        base::log10(.data[["n_records"]] + 1)
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    data_corrections_current |>
      dplyr::select(
        "taxon_name",
        "trait_domain_name"
      ) |>
      dplyr::mutate(already_corrected = TRUE),
    by = dplyr::join_by(taxon_name, trait_domain_name)
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    already_corrected = tidyr::replace_na(.data[["already_corrected"]], FALSE)
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(
    .data[["trait_domain_name"]],
    dplyr::desc(.data[["priority_score"]])
  )

# Apply domain filter.
if (
  !base::is.null(sel_domain_filter)
) {
  data_flagged <-
    data_flagged |>
    dplyr::filter(
      .data[["trait_domain_name"]] == sel_domain_filter
    )
}

# Apply page cap.
if (
  !base::is.null(sel_max_pages)
) {
  data_flagged <-
    data_flagged |>
    utils::head(sel_max_pages)
}
```

```{r}
#| label: generate-pages
#| results: 'asis'

base::set.seed(900723)

vec_domains <-
  data_flagged |>
  dplyr::pull(.data[["trait_domain_name"]]) |>
  base::unique()

purrr::walk(
  .x = vec_domains,
  .f = ~ {
    sel_domain <- .x

    base::cat(
      stringr::str_glue("\n\n## {sel_domain}\n\n")
    )

    data_domain <-
      data_flagged |>
      dplyr::filter(
        .data[["trait_domain_name"]] == sel_domain
      )

    purrr::walk(
      .x = base::seq_len(base::nrow(data_domain)),
      .f = ~ {
        i <- .x

        data_row <-
          data_domain |>
          dplyr::slice(i)

        sel_taxon <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["taxon_name"]])

        n_records_i <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["n_records"]])

        n_outliers_i <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["n_suspected_outliers_taxon"]]) |>
          base::as.integer()

        outlier_fraction_i <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["outlier_fraction"]])

        iqr_i <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["IQR"]])

        mean_median_ratio_i <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["mean_median_ratio"]])

        cv_pct_i <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["cv_pct"]])

        priority_i <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["priority_score"]])

        already_corrected_i <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["already_corrected"]])

        # Outlier direction (ALL HIGH / ALL LOW / MIXED).
        data_group_raw <-
          data_traits_raw |>
          dplyr::filter(
            .data[["taxon_name"]] == sel_taxon,
            .data[["trait_domain_name"]] == sel_domain
          )

        data_group_summary <-
          data_row |>
          dplyr::select(
            "mean",
            "median",
            "IQR",
            "sd",
            "n_records",
            "n_suspected_outliers_taxon",
            "outlier_fraction"
          )

        vec_vals <-
          data_group_raw |>
          dplyr::pull(.data[["trait_value"]])

        q1_i <-
          stats::quantile(vec_vals, probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)

        q3_i <-
          stats::quantile(vec_vals, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)

        iqr_calc <-
          base::unname(q3_i - q1_i)

        upper_fence <-
          base::unname(q3_i + 1.5 * iqr_calc)

        lower_fence <-
          base::unname(q1_i - 1.5 * iqr_calc)

        vec_outlier_vals <-
          vec_vals[vec_vals > upper_fence | vec_vals < lower_fence]

        direction <-
          dplyr::case_when(
            base::all(vec_outlier_vals > upper_fence) ~ "ALL HIGH",
            base::all(vec_outlier_vals < lower_fence) ~ "ALL LOW",
            .default = "MIXED"
          )

        # Heuristic recommendation.
        list_rec <-
          heuristic_recommendation(
            n_records = n_records_i,
            outlier_fraction = outlier_fraction_i,
            n_outliers = n_outliers_i,
            iqr_val = iqr_i,
            mean_median_ratio = mean_median_ratio_i,
            direction = direction
          )

        rec_label <-
          purrr::chuck(list_rec, "label")

        rec_colour <-
          purrr::chuck(list_rec, "colour")

        rec_suggestion <-
          purrr::chuck(list_rec, "suggestion")

        # ---- Plot: raw distribution -------------------------
        plot_dist <-
          plot_trait_group_distribution(
            data_group_raw = data_group_raw,
            data_group_summary = data_group_summary,
            sel_taxon = sel_taxon,
            sel_domain = sel_domain,
            graphical_options = graphical_options,
            verbose = FALSE
          )

        # ---- Plot: family comparison ------------------------
        data_family_comparison <-
          get_family_trait_summary(
            data_traits_raw = data_traits_raw,
            data_classification = data_classification,
            sel_taxon = sel_taxon,
            sel_domain = sel_domain,
            verbose = FALSE
          )

        plot_family <-
          plot_family_trait_comparison(
            data_family_comparison = data_family_comparison,
            data_group_summary = data_group_summary,
            sel_taxon = sel_taxon,
            sel_domain = sel_domain,
            sel_min_n = sel_min_n_family,
            graphical_options = graphical_options,
            verbose = FALSE
          )

        # ---- Panel: stats + heuristic label -----------------
        corrected_text <-
          dplyr::if_else(
            already_corrected_i,
            "YES  [already corrected]",
            "no"
          )

        pct_i <-
          base::round(outlier_fraction_i * 100, 1)

        stats_text <-
          stringr::str_glue(
            "n = {n_records_i}  |  ",
            "outliers = {n_outliers_i} ({pct_i}%)  |  ",
            "direction = {direction}  |  ",
            "CV = {base::round(cv_pct_i, 1)}%  |  ",
            "mean/median = {base::round(mean_median_ratio_i, 2)}  |  ",
            "priority = {base::round(priority_i, 3)}  |  ",
            "corrected = {corrected_text}"
          )

        plot_stats <-
          ggplot2::ggplot() +
          ggplot2::xlim(0, 1) +
          ggplot2::ylim(0, 1) +
          ggplot2::theme_void() +
          ggplot2::annotate(
            "rect",
            xmin = -Inf,
            xmax = Inf,
            ymin = -Inf,
            ymax = Inf,
            fill = rec_colour,
            alpha = 0.15
          ) +
          ggplot2::annotate(
            "text",
            x = 0.5,
            y = 0.65,
            label = rec_label,
            size = 7,
            fontface = "bold",
            colour = rec_colour
          ) +
          ggplot2::annotate(
            "text",
            x = 0.5,
            y = 0.35,
            label = rec_suggestion,
            size = 4,
            colour = "grey30"
          ) +
          ggplot2::annotate(
            "text",
            x = 0.5,
            y = 0.1,
            label = stats_text,
            size = 3,
            colour = "grey40"
          )

        # ---- Compose with patchwork -------------------------
        # wrap_plots() avoids S7 operator dispatch issues that
        #   arise when combining ggview-classed plots with | .
        plot_row_top <-
          patchwork::wrap_plots(
            plot_dist, plot_family,
            ncol = 2L
          )

        plot_combined <-
          plot_row_top /
          plot_stats +
          patchwork::plot_layout(heights = c(0.70, 0.30)) +
          patchwork::plot_annotation(
            title = stringr::str_glue(
              "{sel_taxon}  \u2014  {sel_domain}"
            ),
            subtitle = stringr::str_glue(
              "Heuristic: {rec_label}  |  Suggestion: {rec_suggestion}"
            ),
            theme = ggplot2::theme(
              plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(
                size = 14,
                face = "bold"
              ),
              plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(
                size = 10,
                colour = rec_colour
              )
            )
          )

        base::print(plot_combined)

        base::cat("\n\n\\newpage\n\n")
      }
    )
  }
)
```
